macro_summary: AI infrastructure spend remains the dominant market narrative heading into a busy summer earnings calendar, but rate-hike overhang and a broad semiconductor selloff on July 1 injected near-term volatility. FOMC decision day falls on July 28, coinciding with Microsoft's earnings — a dual macro/micro risk event. Valuation re-ratings in AI-levered names (both domestic and international listings) are the primary opportunity set across all six overnight screens.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs were analyzed over the past 24 hours. The minimum appearance threshold to qualify was 2/6 runs. All 4 qualifying ideas cleared at 5/6 or 6/6, indicating exceptionally strong overnight consensus.
key_events: July 10: SK Hynix (SKHY) tentative Nasdaq ADR trading debut — largest ADR listing in history at ~$29.4B
July 28: Microsoft (MSFT) Q4 FY2026 earnings after close — same day as FOMC rate decision
July 30: Apple (AAPL) Q3 FY2026 earnings after close — consensus revenue $108.86B
August 26: NVIDIA (NVDA) Q2 FY2027 earnings after close — Q2 revenue guided to $91B
July 1 semi selloff: Broad sector dip with no NVDA-specific negative news — flagged as entry opportunity across multiple screens

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Microsoft appeared in all 6 overnight screens — the strongest consensus signal of the session. The stock has de-rated from 30x to 22x forward earnings despite 18% revenue growth and an AI annual run rate exceeding $37B (up 123% YoY), creating a quantifiable valuation gap vs. the consensus analyst price target of $562 (~33% above current ~$422). A confirmed July 28 earnings print with Azure guided at 39–40% CC growth provides the hard catalyst; a defined-risk August bull call spread is the preferred vehicle given FOMC decision noise on the same day.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 28, 2026 after close — coincides with FOMC decision day
ideas.edge: Stock ~$130 below consensus PT of $562 (33% gap); P/E compressed to 22x on 18% revenue growth; last quarter EPS beat of +5.43% and revenue beat of +1.98%; EPS consensus $4.24 with FY2027 EPS consensus $19.09 implying continued re-rating potential
ideas.bull_case: Azure 39–40% CC growth guidance, a multi-quarter beat cadence, and CapEx >$40B signaling AI infrastructure conviction all support a post-earnings re-rating back toward analyst consensus targets; 97 analysts rate Buy or Strong Buy.
ideas.bear_case: FOMC rate decision on the same day (July 28) could overwhelm a beat with macro hawkishness; any Azure growth deceleration below 39% would likely reprice the AI premium sharply.

ideas.name: SK Hynix (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SK Hynix appeared in all 6 overnight screens, driven by its tentative July 10 Nasdaq ADR debut — the largest ADR listing in history at ~$29.4B. The core thesis is a 'Korea Discount' removal: SKHY trades at a single-digit forward P/E vs. NVIDIA at 40x+, despite commanding 57% global HBM market share and posting Q1 2026 operating margins of 72% (exceeding NVIDIA's 65%). HSBC quantifies the re-rating at 20% upside at listing, with P/B target raised from 2.8x to 3.4x and ADS price projected near $166.
ideas.catalyst: Nasdaq ADR trading debut tentatively July 10, 2026 — largest ADR listing in history at ~$29.4B
ideas.edge: HSBC 20% valuation re-rating forecast on Korea Discount removal; single-digit forward P/E vs. NVDA at 40x+; 72% Q1 2026 operating margin vs. NVIDIA's 65%; 57% global HBM market share; no prior US float creates pure accessibility-driven demand at open
ideas.bull_case: Accessibility-driven re-rating on day one, with HSBC's 20% uplift thesis and dominant HBM positioning making this the clearest valuation dislocation in the AI memory space; institutional demand for HBM exposure at a non-NVDA price point is strong.
ideas.bear_case: IPO-day volatility and potential lock-up overhangs could create a buy-the-rumor, sell-the-news dynamic; options are unlikely to be available at listing, limiting hedging ability, and ADR-specific liquidity risks apply in early trading.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVIDIA appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens, with the July 1 broad semiconductor selloff flagged consistently as an entry opportunity rather than a fundamental negative. Q1 FY2027 results showed revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY) and Data Center revenue of $75.2B (+92% YoY), with Q2 FY2027 guided to $91B. The 62-analyst consensus PT of $298.93 vs. current ~$200 implies 49% upside into the August 26 earnings date, and the MGX $49B AI fund announced July 1 signals sustained institutional infrastructure demand. A defined-risk call spread (Aug/Sep expiry) is the consensus preferred vehicle.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings confirmed August 26, 2026 after close; Q2 revenue guided to $91B
ideas.edge: 62-analyst consensus PT of $298.93 vs. ~$200 current price = 49% implied upside; last quarter EPS beat +5.65% and revenue beat +3.04%; gross margin 74.1%; stock dipped ~2% on July 1 semi rout despite no company-specific negative news
ideas.bull_case: AI hyperscaler capex cycle intact, $91B Q2 guidance in place, and the July 1 selloff created an asymmetric entry point with an 8-week runway to earnings — call spreads offer leveraged upside with defined downside.
ideas.bear_case: Rate-hike acceleration or renewed AI-bubble sentiment could weigh on high-multiple semis independent of fundamentals; any supply chain disruption or export control escalation would hit estimates.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Apple appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens, supported by a consistent beat-and-raise cadence heading into July 30 earnings. Last quarter saw EPS beat of +4.15% and revenue beat of +2.09%, with the stock rising 3.69% on the print. The stock is currently ~6% off its 52-week high of $317.40, trading near $298, with a $100B buyback providing a structural floor. A bull call spread into the July 30 print is the consensus preferred vehicle, balancing the moderate-risk profile against macro/rate uncertainty.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings confirmed July 30, 2026; consensus revenue forecast $108.86B
ideas.edge: Last quarter EPS beat +4.15% and revenue beat +2.09% with stock up 3.69% post-print; $100B buyback authorized; 49.3% gross margin on record $29.6B net income; stock ~6% off 52-week high offering a reset entry
ideas.bull_case: Consistent beat cadence, AI-integration narrative (Apple Intelligence), record gross margins, and a $100B buyback backstop create a high-probability setup for a post-earnings move; services revenue diversification reduces hardware cycle risk.
ideas.bear_case: At ~$298 the stock is not cheap on absolute terms, and any iPhone demand miss or China market weakness could offset a services beat; macro rate uncertainty into late July adds headline risk around the same earnings window as MSFT.
